title: Provider × capability matrix
description: Which providers support which payment_method_types + which advanced features (3DS2, manual capture, saved methods, multi-currency, marketplaces). Read by PaymentIntentOrchestrator when routing payments + by PaymentMethodObserver when validating provider/method_type combinations.

stripe.description: Full-feature provider. Widest capability set. Recommended default for new tenants.

paddle.description: Merchant-of-Record provider. Paddle handles taxes + chargebacks + VAT remittance. Ideal for SaaS + digital products.

adyen.description: Global processor + acquirer. Broadest payment method coverage. Marketplace + multi-party splits supported. Recommended for enterprise + global tenants.
adyen.webhook_signature_scheme: HMAC-SHA256 with 'HmacSignature' header over the notification payload

square.description: US-heavy processor. Strong POS integration. Cash App Pay for US consumers.

paypal.description: PayPal Checkout + Braintree (card acquiring) + Venmo (US consumer wallet). Strong recognition + high customer trust.
paypal.webhook_signature_scheme: Certificate + 'Paypal-Transmission-Sig' + 'Paypal-Cert-Url' + 'Paypal-Transmission-Time' + 'Paypal-Auth-Algo' + 'Paypal-Transmission-Id' — verify signature with the cert

custom_webhook.description: Enterprise-only. Escape hatch for tenants running their own payment gateway. All capabilities depend on caller's implementation.
custom_webhook.supported_currencies_note: Empty = tenant declares in their custom_webhook config the currencies they support.

notes: The provider enum + capabilities are the authoritative source. Adding a new provider = adding a new entry here + shipping the driver class + JSON config schema + webhook handler + adding the enum value.

supports_multi_currency=true means the provider accepts non-domicile-currency payments (transacts in the specified currency). supports_multi_currency=false means the tenant is locked to the provider's home currency.
supports_marketplaces=true means the provider supports multi-party splits between the platform + sub-merchants. Required for Stackra's Split Payments feature (future).

supports_setup_intent=true means the provider has a dedicated SetupIntent-equivalent flow for saving methods WITHOUT charging (e.g. saving a card during signup for later use). Stripe SetupIntent, Adyen storedPaymentMethod flow. PayPal + Paddle do not have this — customers save methods via a full first payment.
Fee estimates are TYPICAL DOMESTIC card rates as of 2026-01. Actual fees vary by tenant contract + card type + region. Used only as sanity checks on Payment.fee_cents values reported by the provider.

3DS2 versions 2.1.0 + 2.2.0 are the two currently-active variants. 3DS 1.0.2 is legacy — issuers may reject or downgrade. supports_3ds2_versions documents what each provider will emit in the three_ds_version field of resulting Payment rows.
